manifest.description: Creates a folder at the location of the current note.
manifest.name: Create Folder at Note Location

name: Create Folder at Note Location

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Added a setting that allows setting a file to use as a template. When the "Add folder with note here" command is used and this setting points to a valid file then that file will be copied and renamed in the new folder to be the same name as the folder. If the template file is not filled in or is not valid then an empty file will be created as in versions prior to 1.3.0.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Updated README to add short usage description and gif showing usage.
- Updated files to allow for releasing the plugin with GitHub Actions.

releases.author: Josteniok
releases.description: This release removes the setting to add a note when the folder is created. The capability provided by the setting is replaced with another command that adds a folder along with a note.

releases.author: Josteniok
releases.description:
This Obsidian plugin creates a command that creates a folder at the location of the active note. When the command is run, a modal comes up asking the user for a folder name and then that folder is created at the same location as the active note. If there is no active note then a notification comes up saying that the folder has not been created for this reason. Also, if the name is already in use then a notification comes up indicating that this is why a folder has not been created.

There is also a setting to add a file to the newly created folder with the same name as the folder. This is useful if you use the Waypoint plugin (https://github.com/IdreesInc/Waypoint) since this will automatically add the folder and then the folder note for the folder.
releases.name: Version 1.1.0
